title: Write-behind for financial transactions — async DB write risks silent data loss
expected_feedback: Critical: Write-behind pattern is used for financial transaction persistence, but the async DB write is a fire-and-forget goroutine with no delivery guarantee. If the process crashes, the goroutine is killed, or the DB call fails transiently, the payment record is lost — the caller already received success. This violates the fundamental rule: never use write-behind for financial or audit-critical data (cache-patterns.md section 3). The goroutine uses context.Background(), losing the parent's deadline and trace context. Fix: (1) switch to write-through for financial data — write to DB first, then update cache on success; or (2) if async write is required for throughput, use a durable queue (Kafka, Redis Streams with ACK) between cache and DB so writes survive process crashes. Add idempotency keys to prevent duplicate processing on retry.
